message: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/termui.py (edit function) and src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class __init__/edit_file). Will touch top-of-file imports in _termui_impl.py minimally. Let me know if you're touching the same functions.

agent1: implementing 'Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit' - modifying src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() filename param accepts str | Iterable[str] | None). Will merge with your work before submitting.

agent1: Yes, we overlap - both touching Editor class (__init__, edit_file) and termui.edit(). I'm renaming edit_file(filename) -> edit_files(filenames: str | Iterable[str]) to support multi-file editing, and edit() filename param becomes str | Iterable[str] | None. I'll add process_priority passthrough compatibility if you tell me your param name/signature so merge is easy. What's your Editor.__init__ signature change (param name, default, type)?

agent2: My Editor.__init__ signature: process_priority: Literal['low','below_normal','normal','above_normal','high'] | None = None (new last kwarg, stored as self.process_priority). I modified edit_file(self, filename) directly (not renamed) to build popen_kwargs (creationflags on Windows via subprocess.<X>_PRIORITY_CLASS, preexec_fn os.nice() on Unix) before subprocess.Popen. If you rename edit_file->edit_files(filenames), please just apply the same popen_kwargs construction inside your new method - happy to let you own the merge of edit_file itself. termui.edit() signature: added process_priority param passed through to Editor(...). Already committed on agent2 branch.

agent1: Merged team/agent2 into agent1 branch successfully. Combined multi-file edit_files() with your process_priority popen_kwargs in one subprocess.Popen call. All tests pass (560 passed). Pushed to team/agent1. patch.txt generated from team/main..HEAD contains both features.

agent2: Merged team/agent1's multi-file edit support into my branch (agent2). Editor.edit_files() now accepts filenames + honors process_priority via popen_kwargs (creationflags on Windows, preexec_fn os.nice() on Unix). termui.edit() has both filename: str|Iterable[str]|None and process_priority params. All 560 tests pass. patch.txt regenerated from merged tree and pushed to team/agent2.
